differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma (bone marrow carcinomatosis) leading to myelophthisis
Primary hematologic malignancy (e.g., myelodysplastic syndrome or occult acute leukemia)
Acute pancreatitis, possibly due to underlying malignancy or local obstruction
Occult solid tumor malignancy (e.g., pancreatic or breast carcinoma)
next_steps: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and specialized staining for metastatic epithelial markers (e.g., cytokeratins)
Measurement of serum ADAMTS13 activity to definitively rule out TTP
Full HLH diagnostic workup, including soluble IL-2 receptor, triglycerides, and fibrinogen
Urgent oncology consultation for management of likely metastatic disease
Repeat staging imaging (e.g., PET/CT) to identify the primary tumor source
Initiation of supportive care and early goals-of-care discussions with the patient and family
